subject: Math

lesson.story_mission: Help the Elevation Expedition map temperatures, sea levels, distances from zero, and signed coordinates.
lesson.objective: I can understand positive and negative numbers, opposites, absolute value, integer comparison, and coordinate plane signs.
lesson.concept_explanation: Integers extend the number line in both directions from zero. Opposites are the same distance from zero on different sides, and absolute value is distance from zero. Coordinates use signed x and y values.
lesson.common_misconception: A common mistake is thinking a larger digit always means a greater integer, even when the number is negative.
lesson.real_world_connection: Temperatures below zero, elevations below sea level, credits and debts, and maps use positive and negative numbers.
lesson.lesson_summary: Use zero as the anchor, compare positions from left to right, and read coordinate signs carefully.

page_audio.label: Read This Page
page_audio.text: Read This Page. Integers include negative numbers, zero, and positive numbers. On a number line, numbers farther right are greater. Absolute value is distance from zero, so negative five and positive five both have an absolute value of five.

worked_examples.text: Negative 4 is left of 0 and positive 3 is right of 0, so negative 4 is less than 3.

page_audio.label: Read This Page
page_audio.text: Read This Page. Watch the signed number line. Negative four is four units left of zero, and positive 3 is three units right of zero. A point farther left is less, so negative four is less than 3.

worked_examples.text: The absolute value of -6 is 6 because -6 is 6 units from zero.

page_audio.label: Read This Page
page_audio.text: Read This Page. Watch the absolute value model. Start at zero and count the distance to negative 6. Distance is never negative, so the absolute value of negative 6 is 6.

hint_ladder: For comparisons, the number farther right is greater.
For coordinates, read x first, then y, including the signs.

misconception_bank.negative_number_order_error: Negative numbers were ordered as if they were positive. Farther left means less.
misconception_bank.absolute_value_confusion: Absolute value was treated as the original sign instead of distance from zero.
misconception_bank.opposite_number_confusion: The opposite number was not placed the same distance on the other side of zero.
misconception_bank.coordinate_sign_error: The x-sign or y-sign was read in the wrong direction on the coordinate plane.

game_theme.title: Elevation Expedition
game_theme.name: Elevation Expedition
game_theme.mission_intro: Help the Elevation Expedition map temperatures, sea levels, distances from zero, and signed coordinates.

story.label: Read This Page
story.text: Read This Page. Welcome to Elevation Expedition. You will solve mission problems with models, words, and careful checking. Listen for negative numbers, opposites, absolute value, integer comparisons, and signed coordinates so each answer fits the situation.

lesson.label: Read This Page
lesson.text: Read This Page. This lesson teaches negative numbers, opposites, absolute value, integer comparisons, and signed coordinates. Read the quantities, name the operation, and connect the symbols to the visual model. A good answer explains what the number means.

watch.label: Read This Page
watch.text: Read This Page. Step one, read the situation and mark the values. Step two, use the model to show the operation. Step three, compute and check whether the final answer is reasonable.

demo.label: Read This Page
demo.text: Read This Page. In the demo, build the model before answering. Say the important math words out loud and notice how each part of the picture matches the equation. Then explain the answer in one sentence.

practice.label: Read This Page
practice.text: Read This Page. Practice with one supported question. Tap Read Question to hear the problem, then use the model and type or choose the answer. Check units, signs, and values before moving on.

challenge.label: Read This Page
challenge.text: Read This Page. The challenge asks you to choose a strategy with less support. Tap Read Question, compare the visual to the equation, and avoid common mistakes before submitting.

checkpoint.label: Read This Page
checkpoint.text: Read This Page. The checkpoint shows what you can do independently. Tap Read Question, solve carefully, and use your answer to prove you understand negative numbers, opposites, absolute value, integer comparisons, and signed coordinates.

badge.label: Read This Page
badge.text: Read This Page. You completed the Integers and the Number Line mission. Celebrate the models, explanations, and accurate answers you used today. The badge marks readiness for more Number System work.

profile.label: Read This Page
profile.text: Read This Page. Your profile saves stars, accuracy, hints, and misconception notes. Use Practice This Skill for focused levels and Mixed review so negative numbers, opposites, absolute value, integer comparisons, and signed coordinates stay strong.

level_banks.label: Level 1: Positive and Negative Numbers
level_banks.focus: Represent positive and negative numbers on a number line.
